title: Spergularia clevelandi
taxonomy: Spergularia clevelandi is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It belongs to the genus Spergularia, which is a group of annual or perennial plants commonly known as sand-spurreys or beach-spurreys. The family Caryophyllaceae is a large group of flowering plants that also includes other species such as carnations and pinks. Within the Caryophyllaceae family, Spergularia clevelandi is further classified as being in the order Caryophyllales. This classification helps to organize and understand the evolutionary relationships between different plant species.

intro: Spergularia clevelandi, also known as Cleveland's sandspurry, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family. Traditionally, Cleveland's sandspurry has been used to treat various health issues, including inflammation and respiratory problems, due to its purported anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. Cleveland's sandspurry is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other plants to enhance its therapeutic benefits.
